ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKM covers ready-to-drink black tea; lychee-flavoured variant not separately coded. Closest parent code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code for lychee-flavoured black tea beverage identified in published SNOMED CT hierarchy.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup (approximately 250 ml / 250 g) of lychee black tea without toppings, as typically served at Singaporean bubble tea or speciality tea stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Lychee Black Tea (No Topping) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Lychee Black Tea (No Topping), CR ID D08010068. All per-100g values preserved as reported.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — black tea with sugar, analogous estimate for lychee-flavoured sweetened black tea
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lychee-flavoured black tea. GI of 57 (medium) is estimated by analogy to sweetened black tea / sugar-sweetened tea beverages as reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as (57 × 7.49 × 2.5) / 100 = 10.7 g. Input record GI confidence was 0.31 (low); this analogous estimate is consistent with that low confidence and is documented accordingly.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (7.49 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently raises blood pressure risk; sodium is low at 1 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage, as its 7.49 g sugar per 100 ml exceeds the 5 g/100 ml beverage sugar threshold and added dietary sugar independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines; choose plain unsweetened black tea (teh-o kosong) instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 7.49 g sugar/100 g, zero dietary fibre, and medium GI (57) producing a glycemic load of 10.7 g per standard 250 ml serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid lychee black tea as it is a sugar-sweetened beverage delivering 18.7 g of rapidly absorbed sugar per 250 ml serving with no fibre to blunt the glycemic response; request 0% sugar or substitute with unsweetened black tea per ADA dietary guidelines.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased cardiovascular risk; fat and sodium are negligible but added sugar load is clinically relevant
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage, as habitual SSB consumption is independently associated with increased CVD risk and dyslipidaemia per AHA scientific statements; fat (0 g) and sodium (1 mg/100 g) are not concerns, but the 7.49 g sugar/100 g content is.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar load (7.49 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; no meaningful anti-inflammatory components present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this drink as its added sugar content promotes inflammatory mediators and it provides no anti-inflammatory benefit; replace with plain unsweetened black tea to retain polyphenol benefits without the pro-inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lychee Black Tea (No Topping) is a popular Singaporean speciality tea beverage consisting of freshly brewed black tea flavoured with lychee syrup and sweetened with added sugar, served without any additional toppings such as pearls, jelly, or pudding. It is widely available at bubble tea shops, hawker centres, and speciality tea stalls across Singapore. The drink has a light amber colour, a floral-sweet lychee aroma, and a mildly astringent black tea base balanced by the sweetness of lychee flavouring. Per 100 ml, it provides 29 kcal, 7.49 g of carbohydrate (entirely as sugar), negligible protein (0.01 g), zero fat, zero dietary fibre, and very low sodium (1 mg). The absence of toppings reduces caloric density compared to bubble tea variants but does not alter the fundamental sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) classification. Clinically, this drink is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high sugar content and zero fibre, which results in rapid glycemic excursion; a standard 250 ml serving delivers approximately 18.7 g of sugar and a glycemic load of 10.7. It is also a concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management due to the independent blood-pressure-raising and CVD-risk-increasing effects of regular SSB consumption. The glycemic index is estimated at 57 (medium) by analogy to sweetened black tea. For health-conscious consumers, plain unsweetened black tea (teh-o kosong) is the recommended alternative.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D08010068) with a reported confidence of 0.95. Source match is exact. GI is an analogous estimate (low GI confidence, 0.31) from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021, which is the only approved GI source; this is documented. Overall record confidence is rated high based on the exact HPB match for all macros and micronutrients.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (D08010068); this is a water-based tea beverage containing no fat by formulation — confirmed zero. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by the same rationale. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source; brewed tea with lychee syrup contains no dietary fibre — confirmed zero. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific lychee black tea formulation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D08010068 or in USDA FoodData Central for an equivalent product as of 2026. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate based on sweetened black tea from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for lychee-flavoured black tea was found in approved sources. Input record GI confidence was 0.31 (low), consistent with this analogous estimate. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (57 × 7.49 × 2.5) / 100 = 10.7 g.
